So on Facebook, when you're targeting cold traffic, there is something called interest targeting. So there are interests around Dave Matthews, there are interests around, you know, places that you could go books that you could read. The interests are endless. ## [30:21 - 33:24]

So triggering events create windows of opportunity where your prospect is far more likely to act. So triggering events could be a job change, birth of a child, a marriage, a divorce, a birthday, major system failure, right? Maybe their air conditioning went out. So therefore they need to hire an air conditioning company. Maybe their CRM crash. So they're finally ready to buy another CRM. Holidays, right? Graduation, triggering events could be anything. If you're selling toothbrushes, it might be that the dentist just told them that they need to take better care of their teeth, or it might just be that their current toothbrush is old and they need to buy a new one. But if you start to think about triggering events, if you're selling more of a commodity product that doesn't have a market around it, you start to think about triggering events, it's going to make this a lot easier. And that's where some of the underbrows, some of these categories that I showed you that I said I don't use a lot, they can come into play more for triggering events.
